text: {"challenges":[{"challengeType":"scope-drift","targetKind":"claim","targetIndex":1,"topic":"","rationale":"The source reports results from an eight‑week trial with 42 participants aged 65‑75. Claim [1] extrapolates that the program \"prevents falls permanently for everyone of every age,\" which goes far beyond the sample size, age range, and follow‑up period of the study.","suggestedRemedy":"Restrict the claim to the studied population and timeframe, or cite additional longitudinal, age‑diverse evidence that supports a permanent, universal effect."},{"challengeType":"omitted-qualification","targetKind":"claim","targetIndex":0,"topic":"","rationale":"The original study likely reports statistical significance, confidence intervals, or acknowledges limited power. Claim [0] states the program \"reduced falls\" without noting whether the reduction was statistically significant or the magnitude of effect, thereby dropping a material caveat present in the source.","suggestedRemedy":"Add the study's statistical results (e.g., p‑value, effect size) and any noted limitations, such as small sample size or borderline significance."}]}
